notes:
- Key concepts introduced (with brief explanation)
  - Team (multi-agent system): multiple agents collaborate in a defined workflow (e.g., plot writer, character writer, ending writer); can run in rounds and maintain context.
  - Single-agent team: a team with a single participant that can still run multiple turns/rounds (useful for iterating a single role).
  - Round-robin group chat: team mode where participants take turns in sequence.
  - Selector group chat (selector team): team mode where a selector/“brain” routes tasks to the most appropriate agent based on names/descriptions and prompt instructions.
  - Brain/model client: the selector’s decision-making model used to choose which agent should receive a task.
  - run vs runstream: run returns final result after full execution; runstream yields agent outputs as they complete (streaming).
  - Resetting a team (team.reset): clears team context/history (like starting a new chat).
  - Context persistence: teams keep context across runs unless reset; can resume and continue with retained context.
  - max_turns parameter: limits number of round-robin iterations; default is None (no limit).
  - Termination condition: logic/rules to stop a team when no explicit max_turns is set (important for selector teams too); coming up as next topic.
  - allow_repeated_speaker flag: controls whether the same agent can speak repeatedly in selector teams (can set false to prevent repetition).
  - task_result message type: special result message that contains summary/context of the run (used to detect things like "maximum number of turns reached").
  - Tools per agent: each agent can have its own tools (e.g., planning agent, web search, data analyst).
  - Advanced team types: swarm, “magnetic” (typo in transcript; likely “magnetic”), graph flow — additional interaction patterns beyond round-robin/selector (advanced, out-of-scope for crash course).

- Any analogies or metaphors used
  - Teacher analogy for selector: three teachers (history, geography, maths) and a person outside asking a doubt — selector must route the question to the right teacher.
  - Reset ≈ new chat in ChatGPT (reset removes prior context).
  - Generic agent ≈ general teacher or physician who “thinks he knows everything” (problem of repeated selection).

- Any code, commands, or config shown (literal or paraphrased)
  - Setting max turns example: max_turns = 6 (or 2)
  - team.reset
  - team.run(...)
  - async for message in team.runstream:
      write a short poem about the fall season  (example streamed task)
  - from autogen.agent.chat.base import task_result  (approximate import mentioned)
  - Creating multiple team instances to avoid naming confusion (e.g., team2, team3)
  - Example participant list (conceptual): participants = [planning_agent, web_search, data_analyst]
  - Selector config pieces to supply: participants, brain (model client), termination condition, prompt, name + description for each agent
  - Flags: allow_repeated_speaker = False (to prevent repeated speaker)

- Any warnings, gotchas, or important caveats
  - Default max_turns is None — without a termination mechanism a team can run indefinitely (costly and can hang processes).
  - Selector group chat requires agent names and descriptions for correct routing; omission reduces accuracy.
  - Weak or poor prompting reduces agent-team quality and coordination (e.g., reviewer/writer workflows require strong prompts).
  - Resetting a team deletes context — behaves like a fresh chat.
  - Streaming (runstream) will yield outputs as agents finish; run will wait for final outcome.
  - If you don’t set max_turns or a termination condition, the system may keep running and charge you; in demos this may require restarting the kernel to stop.
  - allow_repeated_speaker must be set thoughtfully to avoid over-selecting a “generic” agent.
  - Advanced team patterns (swarm, magnetic, graph flows) require extra in-depth understanding — out of scope for crash course but available.

- Any comparisons or lists (potential table candidates)
  - Single-agent team vs Multi-agent team
    - Single-agent: simpler, useful for iterating one role, can still run multiple rounds
    - Multi-agent: diverse viewpoints, multiple roles, greater creativity and depth, requires more setup
  - Round-robin group chat vs Selector group chat
    - Round-robin: participants speak in sequence; use max_turns to limit rounds
    - Selector: a router/brain chooses the best agent per task; needs names/descriptions and termination condition
  - run vs runstream
    - run: produce full result after completion
    - runstream: stream agent outputs as they finish (useful for observing incremental outputs)
  - Benefits and trade-offs of team approach (mentioned qualitatively):
    - Creativity: multi-agent > single-agent
    - Viewpoint diversity: multi-agent > single-agent
    - Setup effort: multi-agent > single-agent
    - Depth/flexibility: multi-agent offers more
  - Example team participants mentioned (as a conceptual list): plot writer, character writer, ending writer, planning agent, web search, data analyst

- Direct quotes worth preserving
  - "as Kai and Charmander celebrated their victory over Master Kumra, the burned shadows begin to swirl and reform, revealing a hidden message in the groves, ashes etc."
  - "this ending is something which top plot writer would have uh kind of inculcated because it is having the context and uh the character writer Maybe it would have made it a little bit better based on the plot with the ending."
  - "If you make an error or very weak prompting you will see that they are not behaving nicely."
  - "resetting just delete all the all the context just like uh let's say a new chat in chat GPD where no context is there"
  - "this will be having the full idea that okay what all has happened"
  - "it will keep on running unfortunately because it don't have any max turns it is none by default. So yeah it will happen it will just be running indefinitely and charging you a lot"
  - "name and description become a lot important because from the name and the description only it will be able to tell that yes this is the particular agent whom I should send this request to"
  - "allow_repeated_speaker" (flag name called out explicitly)
  - "maximum number of turns two reached" (example task_result/status message)
